summary: Subarachnoid hemorrhage due to its presence primarily around the cerebral sulci and cisterns without clear signs pointing towards other types like extradural hematoma which would show more distinct localized collections above dura mater; also consistent absence from deep white matter areas ruling out intraparenchymal bleedings while noting that SAH often accompanies traumatic events but here there was none mentioned explicitly supporting non-traumatic causes such aneurysms leading us back again toward likely scenario being subarachnoid haemorrhaege.
